title: Polypodium limapes
intro: Polypodium lepes (also known as Polypodium lepidopterum or Polypodium lepidotis) is a type of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium lepes include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Polypodium lepes include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and support immune function.
taxonomy: Polypodium limapes is a species of the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all ferns. This species is further classified within the order Polypodiales, and the family Polypodiaceae. The genus Polypodium is a group of terrestrial ferns, and Polypodium limapes is one of the species within this genus. The classification of Polypodium limapes is constantly evolving as new research and discoveries are made.
uses: Polypodium limapes, also known as the maidenhair fern, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. It is also believed to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to combat infections. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Polypodium limapes in treating various medical conditions.

These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and have been studied for their potential health benefits. The plant's herbal preparations are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties.
